macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Wall Street Earnings Season Kicks Off Tuesday with Major Banks; CPI Data Expected Tomorrow
items.summary: Earnings season begins in earnest on Tuesday with big banks reporting first, while investors await June CPI data tomorrow amid high investor expectations for corporate America [8].

items.title: S&P 500 Closes Fourth Winning Week in Five as Iran War Drags On; Dow and Nasdaq Nearly Flat
items.summary: The S&P 500 rose to close a fourth winning week in the last five, while the Dow edged up slightly and the Nasdaq remained nearly unchanged as markets weighed Iran war impacts on oil supplies [1].

items.title: Fed Rate Committee Split on Inflation Outlook Amid Iran War Energy Shock
items.summary: Federal Reserve minutes reveal the rate-setting committee is divided on whether inflation will stay elevated or cool once the Iran war ends, with war-driven oil supply uncertainty keeping policymakers on edge [1].

items.title: U.S. Home Prices Hit Record High Despite Slowing Sales; Inflation Pressures Mount
items.summary: U.S. home prices climbed to an all-time high in June despite slowing sales of previously occupied homes, extending three years of annual gains and deepening affordability strains [1].

items.title: Oil Prices Surge as Iran-U.S. Strikes Escalate; Three Tankers Attacked in 24 Hours
items.summary: Oil prices surged following escalated strikes between Iran and the U.S., with three oil tankers attacked within 24 hours and concerns that Iran may be placing naval mines [7][9].

items.title: IMF Slashes 2026 Global Growth Forecast to 3% Amid Inflation and War Uncertainty
items.summary: The IMF now expects global economic expansion to slow to a sluggish 3% in 2026, down from 3.5% in 2025 and its prior 3.1% forecast, due to inflation pressures and the Iran war's energy shock [1].

items.title: U.S. Unemployment Claims Dip to 215,000; Jobless Aid Applications Fall 2,000
items.summary: Applications for U.S. jobless aid dropped by 2,000 to 215,000 in the week ending July 4, as layoffs remain historically low despite cooling hiring [1].

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Holds Near $63,800 Despite War-Driven Selloff in Traditional Markets
items.summary: Bitcoin traded near $63,800 on Monday, July 13, 2026, remaining resilient while gold, oil, equities, and bonds slumped following the U.S.'s fourth round of strikes on Iran in a week[9]. BTC dipped slightly to $62,940 (-1.4% over 24 hours) amid broader market volatility but held above key support near $63,700[1][7].

items.title: U.S. Spot Bitcoin ETFs Record $90.4M Net Inflows Led by BlackRock
items.summary: On July 10, U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs saw $90.4 million in net inflows, with BlackRock’s IBIT leading at $86.8 million and VanEck’s HODL adding $3.6 million[1]. This institutional demand contrasts with Strategy’s recent $216 million BTC sale and supports Bitcoin’s weekly gain of ~1% despite short-term pullbacks[1][3].

items.title: Ethereum Approaches $1,800 as Bulls Test Key Resistance; Volume Drops 12%
items.summary: Ethereum spiked to $1,842 late Sunday and trades around $1,809, testing resistance at $1,820–$1,825 with support at $1,783[2][4]. However, 24-hour trading volume fell 12% to $7.3B, raising questions about rally sustainability despite a short-term golden cross against Bitcoin[2][4].

items.title: Empery Digital Sells 1,400 BTC for $87.1M to Fund AI Data Center
items.summary: Empery Digital liquidated 1,400 BTC ($87.1 million) from its treasury to fund an AI data center project, causing its shares to rise post-sale[4]. The move increases odds of BTC hitting $50K to 46% according to Coinpaper, reflecting corporate treasury shifts amid market uncertainty[4].

items.title: Hong Kong Tightens Crypto Oversight; Ethereum Foundation Dissolves Protocol Team
items.summary: Hong Kong introduced new regulations tightening oversight on crypto asset trading platforms as of July 11, 2026[4]. Meanwhile, the Ethereum Foundation dissolved a key protocol coordination team, signaling a major shift in Ethereum’s multi-year redesign efforts[4].

items.title: XRP Reclaims $1.10 Support; Active Addresses Fall to 2026 Low
items.summary: XRP reclaimed $1.10 support as selling pressure faded, though active addresses dropped to the second-lowest level of 2026 with new wallet creation hitting a 2024 low[4]. The XRP Ledger nears a major upgrade as validator support clears a key threshold, potentially boosting momentum[4].

items.title: Total Crypto Market Cap Sheds $20B; PI and APX Crater by Double Digits
items.summary: The total crypto market cap fell over $20 billion in 24 hours, dropping below $2.24 trillion, while PI and APX prices crashed by double-digit percentages[10]. Bitcoin’s 24-hour decline of 1.29% to $63,000.49 contributed to the broader market contraction[5].

items.title: Bitcoin Holder Kidnapped in Bali for Wallet Keys; Ripple VP Heads to Key Event
items.summary: A Bitcoin holder was kidnapped and tortured for 30 hours in Bali to extract wallet keys, highlighting physical security risks in crypto[4]. Ripple’s Vice President is attending a key event as XRP Ledger momentum builds, with David Schwartz revealing past legal advice nearly ended Ripple before its $150M SEC court fight[4].

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Meta Hires Four OpenAI Researchers Amid ChatGPT Service Disruption
items.summary: Meta has hired four OpenAI researchers—Shengjia Zhao, Jiahui Yu, Shuchao Bi, and Hongyu Ren—while users report ChatGPT being down with issues loading prompts.

items.title: Microsoft Cuts 4,800 Jobs in AI-Driven Restructuring While Investing $190B in Infrastructure
items.summary: Microsoft announced layoffs of approximately 4,800 employees as part of an AI-driven restructuring, coinciding with a $190 billion investment in AI infrastructure this year.

items.title: Anthropic Launches Claude Sonnet 5 and Revives Claude Fable 5
items.summary: Anthropic released Claude Sonnet 5 and brought back Claude Fable 5, making its most capable models more accessible as the AI industry shifts from a chatbot race to broader capabilities.

items.title: Apple to Open Underlying AI Technology to Developers
items.summary: Apple announced plans to open up its underlying AI technology to third-party developers, expanding access to its core AI capabilities.

items.title: SpaceXAI Plans New Model Launch with Cursor by Wednesday
items.summary: SpaceXAI is scheduled to launch a new AI model integrated with Cursor, the AI code editor, potentially as early as Wednesday.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Sony Confirms Significant Bungie Layoffs Including Most of Destiny Team
items.summary: Sony has confirmed major layoffs at Bungie, affecting most of the Destiny team and some Marathon members, as the studio admits Destiny 2 fell short of expectations and cannot continue operating at its current scale.

items.title: Marathon Player Counts Hit All-Time Lows as Bungie Struggles to Rebuild Base
items.summary: Bungie's upcoming game Marathon is facing critical challenges with player counts at all-time lows, raising questions about whether the studio can sustainably rebuild its player base.

items.title: G2 Esports Achieves First International League of Legends Title with Reverse Sweep
items.summary: G2 Esports defeated Top Esports in a historic reverse sweep to win their first international League of Legends title, marking the sixth reverse sweep in international LoL history.

items.title: NBA 2K League Season 2 Week 3 Features Brooklyn Nets Gaming Using Best Build
items.summary: The NBA 2K League continues its Season 2 with Week 3 matches, where Brooklyn Nets Gaming competed using the game's best build in NBA 2K26.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Samsung Unveils Galaxy Z TriFold, Its Most Advanced Foldable Phone Yet
items.summary: Samsung debuted the Galaxy Z TriFold, featuring a redesigned dual-hinge system, upgraded materials, and a 10-inch display that unfolds from a pocketable frame, marking its most advanced foldable device.

items.title: BT and Verizon Merge Global Enterprise Networking Businesses
items.summary: BT and Verizon announced a merger of their global enterprise networking operations, creating a single company serving over 180 countries with a strategic push toward AI-ready infrastructure.

items.title: Microsoft Cuts Nearly 5,000 Jobs While Investing $190B in Infrastructure
items.summary: Microsoft announced layoffs of nearly 5,000 employees while simultaneously pouring $190 billion into infrastructure this year, signaling a major industry shift in resource allocation.

items.title: Huawei Claims New Kirin 2026 Chip Offers 55% More Computing Power
items.summary: Huawei revealed engineering data for its new Kirin 2026 chip, claiming a 55% increase in computing power achieved through smarter design techniques including LogicFolding.

items.title: Quantum Motion Deploys First Full-Stack Quantum Computer Using Standard Silicon
items.summary: Quantum Motion deployed the first full-stack quantum computer built using standard silicon chip fabrication, a milestone expected to accelerate the development of scalable quantum systems.

items.title: Geely's Gold Brick EV Battery Targets 621,000-Mile Lifespan
items.summary: Geely announced its Gold Brick EV battery reaches a 1,093 kW peak power and targets a lifespan of 621,000 miles, representing a significant advancement in electric vehicle energy storage.

items.title: Xbox Gamer Wins Lawsuit Against Microsoft to Restore Account
items.summary: A court ruled that Microsoft must restore a gamer's account and digital library, ordering the company to pay approximately $400 in damages to the user.

items.title: Netflix, Paramount, and Sony in Talks to Acquire Letterboxd
items.summary: Major entertainment companies including Netflix, Paramount, and Sony are reportedly negotiating to purchase the movie-centric social network Letterboxd, which is seeking new ownership.
